ddls:
CREATE TABLE "attributes" (
  "attribute_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attribute */,
  "logical_name" TEXT /* Logical name used in the codebase */,
  "schema_name" TEXT /* Schema name used in the database */,
  "display_name" TEXT /* Display name shown to the end-user */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the attribute (e.g., Lookup, String, DateTime) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attribute */,
  "custom_attribute" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute has custom properties */,
  "required_level" TEXT /* Level of requirement for the attribute */,
  "additional_data" TEXT /* Additional data or notes about the attribute */,
  "data_length" INTEGER /* Maximum length of the attribute value for string types */,
  "data_precision" INTEGER /* Precision for numeric types */,
  "data_scale" INTEGER /* Scale for numeric types */,
  "is_read_only" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is read-only */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the attribute */,
  "validation_rule" TEXT /* Validation rule for the attribute */,
  "is_searchable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is searchable */,
  "is_filterable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is filterable */,
  "is_indexed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is indexed */,
  "is_unique" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute values must be unique */,
  "is_system_attribute" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is a system attribute */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attribute_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attributes_required_level FOREIGN KEY ("required_level") REFERENCES attribute_requirements ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attributes_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES attribute_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attribute_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attribute type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the attribute type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attribute type */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Underlying data type (e.g., INT, VARCHAR, DATE) */,
  "is_system_type" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute type is a system type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attribute_requirements" (
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each requirement level */,
  "requirement_name" TEXT /* Name of the requirement level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the requirement level */,
  "is_system_requirement" INTEGER /* Indicates if the requirement level is a system requirement */,
  "is_client_mandatory" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is mandatory for clients */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("requirement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attribute_custom_properties" (
  "custom_property_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each custom property */,
  "attribute_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attribute the custom property belongs to */,
  "property_name" TEXT /* Name of the custom property */,
  "property_value" TEXT /* Value of the custom property */,
  "is_system_property" INTEGER /* Indicates if the custom property is a system property */,
  "property_description" TEXT /* Description of the custom property */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("custom_property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attribute_custom_properties_attribute_id FOREIGN KEY ("attribute_id") REFERENCES attributes ("attribute_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attribute_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "attribute_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attribute that was changed */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  "changed_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the change was made */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the attribute */,
  "is_major_version" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is a major version */,
  "previous_version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the previous version of the attribute */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attribute_versions_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attribute_versions_attribute_id FOREIGN KEY ("attribute_id") REFERENCES attributes ("attribute_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA, support) */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has admin privileges */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp of the user */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_modified_on" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last modified */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
